query_normalized: unveiling hidden correlations between processing parameters and mechanical properties in additively manufactured alloys materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by measuring the correlation between a specific processing parameter (extrusion force) and a resulting geometric/mechanical outcome (line width) in material extrusion additive manufacturing, which is a subset of the user's broader inquiry into processing parameters and properties in additively manufactured materials.
